title: Reading and Use of English - Part 6
description: You are going to read an article from which six sentences have been removed. Choose from the sentences A–G the one which fits each gap (37–42). There is one extra sentence which you do not need to use.

content.title: Pieces of the Puzzle: Inside Competitive Solving
content.text: To the casual observer, a jigsaw puzzle is a rainy-afternoon pastime. To Thomas Wellard, it is a competitive sport demanding physical stamina, visual acuity and a highly trained mind. Wellard is a three-time national speed-puzzling champion, and he wants the world to know that his hobby deserves more respect. (37) [37] Speed puzzlers train for months before major tournaments, studying techniques for sorting, edge-finding and pattern recognition. [/37] || The world of competitive puzzling encompasses far more than jigsaws. Crossword tournaments, Rubik's Cube speed-solving — known as 'speedcubing' — and logic puzzle championships all draw large, devoted followings. (38) [38] What unites all these disciplines is an emphasis on mental agility, concentration and the ability to perform under pressure. [/38] The World Puzzle Championship, held annually since 1992, attracts competitors from over forty countries who solve dozens of different puzzle formats against the clock. || Training regimes vary by discipline. Speedcubers practise specific sequences of moves, called algorithms, until their fingers execute them without conscious thought. (39) [39] Crossword solvers, by contrast, focus heavily on vocabulary breadth and the ability to recognise wordplay patterns instantly. [/39] Top competitors in either field can spend three or four hours a day on deliberate practice. || Mental fatigue is a significant issue in long competitions. (40) [40] Experienced competitors develop personal strategies for maintaining focus during multi-hour events, such as controlled breathing or brief visualisation exercises. [/40] Some wear noise-cancelling headphones; others prefer total silence in the competition hall. || The communities surrounding these sports are notably welcoming to newcomers. Online forums, tutorial videos and beginner-friendly competitions make it easy to start. (41) [41] Many top competitors actively mentor beginners, reflecting a culture that values participation as much as victory. [/41] || Whether you are drawn to the tactile satisfaction of interlocking cardboard, the mechanical click of a cube's final move or the flash of insight that fills in a difficult clue, competitive puzzling offers something for everyone. (42) [42] The only requirement is a willingness to embrace the challenge — and perhaps a great deal of patience. [/42]

paragraphs.A: A single error under competition conditions can undo minutes of careful work.
paragraphs.B: Unlike many competitive pursuits, puzzling tends to foster cooperation rather than rivalry.
paragraphs.C: The physical component of puzzling is often underestimated by those who have never competed.
paragraphs.D: Each speciality rewards a slightly different set of cognitive skills.
paragraphs.E: This reflects the discipline's deep roots in logical thinking and systematic problem-solving.
paragraphs.F: The barrier to entry is low, but the ceiling of achievement is extraordinarily high.
paragraphs.G: Managing this effectively often separates good competitors from great ones.

questions.explanation: C menciona que el componente físico del puzzling es subestimado, lo que refuerza la idea de que merece más respeto como deporte.
D señala que cada especialidad recompensa habilidades cognitivas distintas, conectando con la lista de disciplinas mencionadas antes del hueco.
E hace referencia a las raíces lógicas y sistemáticas del solving, contrastando con el enfoque más lingüístico de los crucigramistas.
G afirma que gestionar la fatiga separa a los buenos de los grandes competidores, conectando directamente con el párrafo sobre el cansancio mental.
B indica que el puzzling fomenta la cooperación en lugar de la rivalidad, apoyando la descripción de las comunidades acogedoras.
F resume que la barrera de entrada es baja pero el techo de logro es alto, completando el mensaje de que hay algo para todos.
